feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Extended Warehouse Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to change the serial number user status RECG
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently facing an issue with a serial number status. In IQ02, one of the serial numbers is stuck with the user status RECG (“Product Recognition”), and we have been unable to remove or change this status despite trying multiple approaches.

Could you please advise where and how this status can be modified or removed?

Thanks,

Vinod

entry.title: Change an HU number range from internal to external?
entry.content:
What steps should be performed to change an HU number range from internal to external?

Between all the HU ranges configured in my warehouse, i selected an external one to be internal and test a process. This can't be undone, thus the message says that to delete it, the counter should be initial, therefore, that rage can no longer be used for external HU's. I seek to reset it as external Instead of re-configuring a new number range.

Does the counter resets by deleting HU's? I don't think so. 

Thank you all beforehand.

entry.title: Automatic block stock SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there a possibility to change / block stock of certain articles when they are internaly moved to a certain storage bin , or when a special process type is being used. The goal is to change stock type from unrestriced to blocked. 

Target system: SAP EWM decentral

entry.title: LOSC Putaway: Second WT Gets Cancelled After Confirming First WT
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am testing the LOSC process for putaway in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - EWM.

Configuration

  • GR Zone → Intermediate Storage Type → Putaway Storage Type

  • Created two storage types:

    • Intermediate Storage Type

    • Putaway Storage Type

  • Configured the required storage bins and bin determination.

  • Configured a simple LOSC process.

Test Scenario

After posting the Goods Receipt,  created the putaway Warehouse Task.

As expected, the system created two Warehouse Tasks:

  • First WT – Open

  • Second WT – Waiting

However, after confirming the first WT, the second WT is cancelled automatically, and the HU remains stuck in the intermediate storage bin 

Comparison with Outbound LOSC

For the outbound LOSC scenario, I used the same intermediate storage type with the following process:

Putaway Storage Type → Intermediate Storage Type → GI Zone

In this case, the system also created two Warehouse Tasks. After confirming both tasks, the HU moved to the GI Area successfully.

I also tested an outbound LOSC process with multiple intermediate storage types, and it worked as expected.

The issue occurs only during the putaway LOSC process.

Has anyone encountered this behavior before, or is there any additional configuration required for inbound LOSC in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: Generate Warehouse Tasks
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you help us with the following theme, please?

We have a periodic job, program /SCWM/R_DLV_PDO_CREATE_WT (execution variant “FLUXO_CHAO_TDS”), the objective is to generate Warehouse Tasks for a specific delivery type(ZTRA). Our problem is with the sequence of selection of the deliveries(it doesn’t follows a number or date sequence of the deliveries), also it generates tasks in a non-arbitrary logic.

 

For example, he have 10 deliveries(from 1 to 10, all of them have the same dat), the program generates tasks for delivery 2, then goes to delivery 5, 8, then delivery , which means it doesn’t take a sequence. Also the tasks aren’t generated in the sequence of the delivery, it generates some tasks for delivery 2(doesn’t generates tasks for all the quantity), then goes for delivery 5(again generates tasks, but not for all the quantity), then goes to delivery 8(does the same thing),

For more details can you check the attached word document.

Thank you, best regards.

Diana Arana

entry.title: while creating physical inventory facing the message: No storage bin selected for clearing
entry.content: while creating physical inventory facing the transaction LI20

entry.title: How to print a custom HU label from Warehouse Monitor app SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
We are trying to print a custom Handling Unit (HU) label from the Warehouse Monitor app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition EWM.

We created a custom form template and assigned it to the standard HU_LABEL output type in Assign Form Templates.

However, when we click Display Document after selecting Print HU Label, the document rendering fails with the following error:

"Rendering of document failed."

Could you please advise what is causing this error and how it can be resolved?

Please find the attached screenshot for reference.

entry.title: 67332 67343 - WT against 2 OBD
entry.content: 67332 67343 this are the Two warehouse Task, assign for same location same material, but 64332 warehouse task is create for 80006743 order and 67343 is create for 80006742 Order. as Picker start Picking on outbound order basis due to Even same location has Enough QTy still did partial picking.

entry.title: I am getting Queue Error saying Matrial XXXX VG10 AFS Dose not exist.
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP EWM - after confirming the warehouse order my material is not extending to AFS.

Once i do GR in /SCWM/PRDI Stocks get updated in ROD and When i create a warehouse Task and then Confirm the Warehouse Order stocks are not getting updated to AFS. 

When i go to SMQ1 & SMQ2 i find the Queue Error Saying : Material XXXX  VG10  AFS Dose not exist. 
and i solved it through extending the AFS in MMSC t-code. but every time its big problem to do. Is there any other solution or any configuration i need to do ? 
Please guide me When every i cretae new material i am facing this issue. 

VG10 is my Plant.

ROD is my Storage Location for GR-ZONE

AFS is my Final Destination Storage location

 



I usualy go to MMSC -code and maintain the AFS as show in below image 

I appriciate if someone can guide me on this issue to fix.

Thanks in Advance

Regards 

Vinay

entry.title: Usage of I002/O002 in EWM causing queue stuck in ECC SMQ2
entry.content:
System : ECC/S4HANA(cloud) connecting with(side car) EWM (S42023 On Premise)

For Inbound or outbound deliveries, when the process code I002/O002 is used for reducing the delivery quantity, due to the delivery split, EWM processing gets completed.

On ECC side the queue is getting stuck

Request : Without inactivating I002/O002 in the configuration, is there any way we can restrict the usage of these codes using standard functionality?

EWM Inbound delivery process code used

SAP Standard will create new delivery with I002/O002 process code, which is delivery split functionality

In this example business zeroed the quantity and closed the 2nd delivery, the new delivery will get new LE delivery reference.

Due to above process in EWM, ECC side in SMQ2 we see its stuck queue due to delivery split

Detailed log of the queue failure.

Appreciate for your valuable inputs shared.

Thanks,

Hari

entry.title: Enable barcode scanning for Product field (/SCWM/S_RF_SELECTION-RFPROD) in Stock/Bin query product
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement to enable GS1/EAN128 barcode scanning for the Product field in the RF "Stock/Bin Query by Product" transaction.

Expectation - To scan a  barcode string containing Product and Batch information into the Product field. The application should then decode the barcode and populate the Product and Batch fields.

Current Scenario - The Product field refers to /SCWM/S_RF_SELECTION-RFPROD which has conversion exit. When a barcode string is longer than 40 characters, the value is truncated before further processing.

I checked for notes but did not found anything relating this.
2863559 - Error /SCWM/RF_EN195 by RF "Stock / Bin Query by Product" product barcode scan - this was closer but not of much help.

Is there any SAP Note, enhancement, or recommended standard approach to support this requirement? If not, is the recommended solution to create a custom RF program/screen with a barcode input field (without a conversion exit).

Any guidance or references to relevant SAP Notes would be appreciated.

Thank you.

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: S4 Advanced Returns Management - Logistical Follow-Up Actions
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

Happy Friday. I need some guidance regarding SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM).

In standard SAP, the Returns Overview for Customer Returns (transaction MSR_TRC_C) displays the standard follow-on material documents as Logistical Follow-Up Actions resulting from the inspection process (for example, blocked stock to unrestricted stock, blocked stock to returns stock, etc.).

In our scenario, however, the follow-on material documents are posted from decentralized EWM using non-standard processes (using Badi in EWM), such as:

  • Material-to-material transfer (movement types 309/343)
  • Immediate scrapping from blocked stock via PGI (movement type 555)

These postings create the corresponding material documents in S/4HANA.

The issue is that these material documents are not displayed as Logistical Follow-Up Actions in MSR_TRC_C. As a result, the processing status of the Logistical Follow-Up Actions remains yellow (Process Incomplete) instead of changing to green (Document Complete), even though the required material postings have been completed and the material documents properly link to the returns delivery/returns sales order.

We have thoroughly reviewed the standard ARM configuration but have not found any configuration option that supports this requirement.

Could you please advise whether there is a standard approach to achieve this functionality? Alternatively, are there any BAdIs, enhancement spots, implicit enhancements, or other extension points that could be used to populate the follow-on material documents and update the processing status accordingly?

Many thanks in advance for your assistance. If you need any additional information about our scenario, I would be happy to provide it.

 

Thanks,

MP

entry.title: Posting period Queue error in GR reversal
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are using Embeded system.
Business has created IBD>> IBD distributed to the WMS system>> GR is done in previous period >> after the period changed user is trying to perform GR reversal. 
A queue got stuck with posting period error ( system is considering the previous month posting period date) , whereas the stock has been cleared from the WH but still exists in S4 system. How to clear the queue and match the stocks in both system.
Note: Business has no restriction on posting the GR with current period.

Thanks and regards,
Saikumar

entry.title: Putaway bin proposal for multiple fixed bins
entry.content:
During the fixed bin putaway scenario, if a product has multiple fixed bins assigned, our standard understanding is that the system proposes the bin that comes first based on the sort sequence.

However, our requirement is that when multiple fixed bins are assigned to a particular product, the bin that was added first in the /scwm/binmat should be proposed for putaway, instead of the bin determined by the sort sequence.

Kindly confirm whether this behavior is supported in standard functionality. Also, please let us know if there is any BAdI or enhancement option available to achieve this requirement.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  • Check the sort sequence maintained for the fixed bins in storage type X.
  • In /SCWM/BINMAT, assign multiple fixed bins to the same product for the particular st type.
  • Ensure the fixed bins are created at different times so that each assignment has a different creation timestamp. In a test case, BIN6 was assigned first, followed by BIN2, BIN4, etc.
  • Create the inbound delivery for the product.
  • Create the putaway Warehouse Task (WT) for the inbound delivery.Observe the destination bin proposed by the system:
  • Actual Result: The system proposes BIN2, which has the smallest sort sequence among the assigned fixed bins.
  • Expected Result: The system should propose BIN6, as it was the first fixed bin assigned to the product in /SCWM/BINMAT, regardless of the sort sequence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: While doing the scrapping user is facing "Deficit of stock issue"
entry.content:
For the  stock when user is trying to scrap the quantity they are facing "Deficit of stock issue".

We are attaching the document with the complete steps.

Can you please look into the issue.

entry.title: Career Roadmap: Seeking the Definitive Learning Order for SAP S/4HANA MM + EWM Consultant/Specialist
entry.content:
I am currently planning my career path to become an SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management) and EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) Consultant (Specialist) . While there is plenty of information on the individual modules, I am struggling to find a definitive "Learning Order" that connects both worlds effectively.

I want to move beyond just "knowing the transactions" to understanding the embedded integration and process design required for a Specialist.

My Ultimate Goal:
To be proficient in end-to-end processes (Procure-to-Pay and Warehouse Operations) within the S/4HANA ecosystem, including handling complex physical logistics.

entry.title: WOCR not work properly
entry.content: Warehouse order is supposed to be created based on batch no, one batch no one WO ,but system does not follow the rule even we have setup in WOCR .

entry.title: Stock discrepancy in WE-ZONE (Storage Type 9010) after complete Putaway confirmation in Embeded EWM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are facing a stock inconsistency issue in our Embeded EWM system related to Inbound Delivery processing and Putaway.

Process Flow:

  1. Inbound Delivery (IBD) is distributed from ERP to EWM (WMS).
  2. Goods Receipt (GR) is posted successfully against the IBD.
  3. Putaway task (Warehouse Task) is created and confirmed for the full quantity.
  4. As per the process design, stock should move completely from the GR/Interim area (WE-ZONE, Storage Type 9010) to the final destination Storage Bin in the destination Storage Type.

Issue:
Even though the Warehouse Task confirmation shows the Putaway as fully completed (100% quantity confirmed, no open/partially confirmed WTs), the physical stock overview (/SCWM/MON) still shows a residual/duplicate quantity lying in Storage Type 9010 (WE-ZONE — interim GR area) with "0" quantity, in addition to the stock correctly reflected in the destination bin.

This is resulting in inflated stock quantity for the material, as the same stock appears to exist in two locations simultaneously — the interim GR Zone (9010) and the final Putaway bin — even though only one physical movement (Putaway) was performed and confirmed.

Steps Already Performed:

  • Verified Warehouse Task confirmation status – shows "Confirmed," no open quantity.
  • Checked Physical Inventory / Stock overview (/SCWM/MON) – quant still exists in 9010.

    Thanks & regards,
    Saikumar
